title: Log Analysis 📜
description: Reading the digital footprints left by an attacker.

slides.title: The Digital Breadcrumbs 📜
slides.content:
### What are Logs?

Almost every action on a computer (login, file access, network connection) is recorded in a **Log File**. 

Learning to read logs is like being a 'digital detective' who can reconstruct exactly how a hacker moved through the system.

slides.title: A Malicious Log ⌨️
slides.content: Look at this web server log entry. Can you see anything suspicious?
slides.codeSnippet:
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Mar/2026] "GET /login.php?user=' OR '1'='1 HTTP/1.1"
# Wait, that's a SQL Injection attempt!

slides.title: SIEM 📊
slides.content:
### Scaling the Logs

A large company produces billions of log lines every day. Humans can't read them all! 

**SIEM** (Security Information and Event Management) is software that collects all logs from every server and uses AI to find the 'needles in the haystack'.

quizData.question: Which tool is used to centralize and analyze log data from many different sources automatically?
quizData.explanation: SIEM platforms (like Splunk or ELK) are the central nervous system of a security operations center.
